forecast-period.text: A high pressure system south of WA extends a ridge over South Australia. This high moves slowly east to be south of the Bight by tonight. The high is forecast to hover over SA tomorrow, then weaken and step back to the west on Tuesday allowing a series of fronts to pass over the southeast on Wednesday and Thursday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 5 metres, tending southerly 4 to 5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
West to southwesterly about 10 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southerly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.
Westerly about 10 knots increasing to 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southerly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending southwesterly 2 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots before dawn.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy.
Westerly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Sunday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Port MacDonnell during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 4 to 6 metres, decreasing to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots south of Port MacDonnell early in the morning. Winds turning westerly in the afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Beachport during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
